name: Current Affairs in History & National Movement (11): Vijayanagara-Era Inscriptions Found at Tirupati

sourceRefs.label: organiser.org - 2 16th-century Vijayanagara inscriptions discovered in Tirupati's Seshachalam forest (announced 27 June 2026)

researchNotes: Thirteenth History current-affairs topic (11th lap). The freshest finding used in this entire project — announced the same day as this topic's authoring (27 June 2026). Names Emperor Sadasiva Deva Raya, a real Vijayanagara ruler, giving this current-affairs item direct syllabus-relevant content (Vijayanagara Empire administration).

reading.intro: Covers the discovery of 2 Vijayanagara-era inscriptions in Tirupati, revealing 16th-century temple administration practices.

qa.question: Where were the 2 Vijayanagara-era inscriptions discovered, and when was this announced?
qa.answer: In the Seshachalam forest region of Tirupati district — announced 27 June 2026, found during a heritage exploration and documentation exercise by researchers and epigraphists.

qa.question: Which Vijayanagara Emperor is named in the inscriptions, and what do they document about his reign?
qa.answer: Emperor Sadasiva Deva Raya (mid-16th century) — the inscriptions document land grants issued during his reign for the maintenance of religious activities at the Parashurameswara Mandir in Gudimallam village.

qa.question: What is significant about the Parashurameswara Temple at Gudimallam itself?
qa.answer: It is renowned for housing one of the OLDEST known Shiva lingas in India — the new inscriptions show the temple continued receiving state patronage centuries after its original foundation, well into the Vijayanagara period.

qa.question: What do the inscriptions reveal about Vijayanagara governance structures?
qa.answer: That land grants required careful documentation, surveying, and administrative oversight — illustrating how imperial authority extended into local communities through networks of officials, village institutions, and temple (Mandir) administrations.

qa.question: What was the practical purpose of the land grants described in the inscriptions?
qa.answer: They provided income through agricultural production to support the temple's daily rituals, offerings, and maintenance.
